questions: Identify the major reference work aimed at providing updated tools for demographic estimation from limited and defective data, which was produced as a joint project between the IUSSP and UNFPA and serves as a direct successor to the UN Manual X. Within this resource, locate the specific section or chapter dedicated to the 'Evaluation and correction of fertility data'. Provide the full suggested citation for this specific section, explicitly listing the author of the section, the publication year, the full list of editors for the parent volume, and the publisher.

rounds.answer_a:
The major reference work is **Tools for Demographic Estimation**, a comprehensive volume developed as a joint IUSSP/UNFPA project that serves as the successor to UN Manual X[1][3]. This resource was designed to address the critical need for updated demographic estimation methods, as classical training in estimation from limited and defective data was rapidly declining among the demographic profession[2].

The work contains a dedicated chapter on **"Evaluation and correction of fertility data"** within its structure[3]. This section addresses systematic errors in fertility data collection, including the under-reporting of lifetime fertility by older women and the under-reporting of recent births across all age groups[2]. A key correction method discussed is the **el-Badry correction**, which specifically addresses errors where enumerators incorrectly code childless women as "parity unknown" rather than zero parity[1].

However, the search results provided do not contain sufficient information to furnish the complete citation details requested, including the specific section author, full editor list, and exact publication year with certainty. The results confirm the work's existence and general publication details but lack the comprehensive bibliographic information needed for a full formal citation. To obtain the precise citation with section author attribution and complete editorial information, consulting the publication directly or the IUSSP's official database would be necessary.

## References

[1] Tools for Demographic Estimation - el-Badry correction. https://demographicestimation.iussp.org/content/el-badry-correction

[2] Methods of fertility estimation – Demographic Anthropology. https://ebooks.inflibnet.ac.in/antp12/chapter/methods-of-fertility-estimation/

[3] Tools for Demographic Estimation [PDF]. https://demographicestimation.iussp.org/sites/default/files/sites/default/filesd6/TDE_2013_2ndImpression_0.pdf
